description_long: This is a large, two-story brick house with a complex, multi-hipped roof in dark gray. The front facade features symmetrical window arrangements, including large arched windows and rectangular panes, and a prominent central entryway with a small overhang supported by columns. The brick exterior is consistent on all sides, with minimal decorative trim and a focus on clean, geometric lines. The roofline is varied, with several dormer-like projections and intersecting hips, giving the structure a stately, substantial appearance. The windows are evenly spaced, and the main entrance is accentuated by a set of steps and a covered porch. The overall design suggests a blend of Colonial and modern suburban architectural influences.
description: A large, two-story brick house with a dark, multi-hipped roof, symmetrical windows, and a prominent central entryway with columns and an overhang.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with columns, symmetrical arched and rectangular windows, and the full width of the house.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long brick wall with minimal window openings and the complex, intersecting hip roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back brick facade, roof projections, and absence of a visible entryway or large windows.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the depth of the house, roof complexity, and the side brick wall with no major features.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entryway with a small porch, columns, steps, and an overhanging roof
